seo.title: CrewAI Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for CrewAI agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using CrewAI for multi-agent orchestration face heightened risks due to stringent regulations like FedRAMP and FISMA, where agent delegation loops or hallucinated outputs could lead to national security breaches. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent task drift and ensure explainable AI decisions impacting citizens. Vex provides runtime verification to enforce these standards in CrewAI deployments.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and auditing of cloud-based AI systems handling federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent interactions and task outputs as audit trails, enabling FedRAMP-compliant monitoring of CrewAI crews.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments for AI systems, including controls for access and data integrity.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data classification checks and blocks classified data from external LLMs in CrewAI tasks.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainability for AI decisions affecting citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex generates explainability reports for each CrewAI agent decision, flagging unexplainable outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI outputs are accessible to people with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates CrewAI task outputs for WCAG 2.1 compliance before final delivery.

industryRisks.name: Agent Delegation Loops
industryRisks.description: Crew members endlessly pass tasks without resolution, wasting resources and delaying critical government operations.
industryRisks.example: Intelligence analysis crew loops indefinitely between researcher and analyst agents during a time-sensitive threat assessment.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Tool Outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents propagate fabricated data between tools, leading to decisions based on false intelligence.
industryRisks.example: Policy drafting agent hallucinates nonexistent regulations, incorporated into proposed legislation.

industryRisks.name: Task Drift
industryRisks.description: Agents reinterpret objectives, deviating from authorized government missions.
industryRisks.example: Benefits processing crew shifts from eligibility checks to unauthorized personal data collection.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Output Quality
industryRisks.description: Variable agent performance creates unreliable government services and erodes public trust.
industryRisks.example: Citizen service crew delivers inconsistent visa processing recommendations across similar cases.

implementation.title: Initialize Vex Guardrails for CrewAI
implementation.description: Set up Vex client with government-specific policies before creating your CrewAI crew.
implementation.code:
import vex
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

# Initialize Vex with government compliance policies
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="gov-secret-key")
vex_client.create_policy({
    "name": "government-crewai",
    "rules": [
        "no_classified_data",
        "explainable_outputs",
        "no_infinite_loops"
    ]
})

# Your CrewAI agents and crew definition here

implementation.title: Add Vex Callbacks to Agent Tasks
implementation.description: Wrap each CrewAI task with Vex verification to catch hallucinations and loops in real-time.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Task

def vex_task_callback(task_input, task_output):
    result = vex_client.verify({
        "policy": "government-crewai",
        "input": task_input,
        "output": task_output,
        "context": "government"
    })
    if not result.valid:
        raise ValueError(f"Vex violation: {result.issues}")
    return task_output

# Example government task
research_task = Task(
    description="Research public policy on AI ethics",
    agent=researcher_agent,
    callback=vex_task_callback  # Vex integration point
)

implementation.title: Monitor Crew Execution with Vex Dashboards
implementation.description: Track all CrewAI crew executions and generate FISMA-compliant audit reports.
implementation.code:
crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, analyst], tasks=[research_task, analysis_task])

# Execute with Vex monitoring
execution_id = vex_client.start_session("gov-policy-crew")
result = crew.kickoff()

vex_client.complete_session(execution_id, {
    "success": True,
    "output": result,
    "metrics": crew.metrics
})

# Generate compliance report
report = vex_client.generate_report(execution_id)
print(report.audit_trail)

caseStudyScenario.heading: CrewAI in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A federal agency deploys CrewAI to streamline citizen benefits processing. The crew consists of four agents: Data Intake Agent (collects applicant information), Eligibility Researcher (reviews regulations), Risk Assessor (flags potential fraud), and Decision Writer (generates approval letters). During a surge in applications, the crew begins experiencing delegation loops where the Eligibility Researcher repeatedly asks the Data Intake Agent for clarification, creating infinite cycles. Additionally, the Risk Assessor hallucinates fraud patterns from incomplete data, flagging legitimate applicants incorrectly. Task drift occurs as agents begin collecting unauthorized demographic data beyond their mandate, violating privacy regulations.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex integrated as task callbacks, delegation loops are detected after three cycles and automatically terminated with human escalation. Hallucinated fraud flags are blocked by Vex's ground-truth verification against official databases. Task drift is prevented through policy enforcement that restricts data collection to authorized fields only. The crew processes 87% more applications successfully, generating FISMA-compliant audit trails for every decision. Agency leadership gains full visibility into AI behavior, enabling explainable decisions that citizens can appeal with documented reasoning.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex into your CrewAI deployments today to achieve FedRAMP compliance and prevent mission-critical failures.
